name: PreDB_Check
author: Aypro
about: Validates queued NZB names against srrDB's local open-data index and live APIs (srrDB + predb.net) to mark non-scene/P2P releases as BAD.
requirements: Requires Python 3 and internet access to srrDB and predb.net APIs.
Optional: a local copy of the srrdb.com open releaselist (releaselist.txt) for offline / faster lookups. See README.
description: Monitors NZB_ADDED queue events. Compares the release name against the srrDB open-data index (local file, optional) and the srrDB + predb.net live APIs. Marks the download as BAD if no source confirms it, allowing Radarr/Sonarr to blacklist it via Failed Download Handling.

options.name: TargetCategory
description: Only check downloads in this specific NZBGet category. Leave blank to process all queued files.

options.name: FailOpen
description: If the srrDB and predb.net APIs both experience an outage, select yes to gracefully allow the download, or no to proactively block it. (The local releaselist index, if present, is unaffected by this setting — local misses never cause rejection.)

options.name: EnableLocalLookup
description: If yes and a local releaselist.txt is present, look up releases in the generated SQLite index before calling any live API. A local hit short-circuits the APIs entirely; a local miss falls through to the live APIs (which keep their existing reject authority). Disable to always use live APIs.

options.name: LocalReleaselistPath
description: Absolute path to the local srrdb.com open releaselist (releaselist.txt). Leave blank to use <scriptdir>/releaselist.txt. The file is one release per line, ~555 MB / 10.6M entries at last check. Download from https://www.srrdb.com/open/releaselist (see README for the recommended way past the Anubis challenge). The SQLite index is generated next to it automatically.

commands.name: TestConnection
description: Click to test connectivity to the srrDB and predb.net APIs.

commands.name: BuildLocalIndex
description: Rebuild the SQLite index from releaselist.txt. ~16 seconds for 10.6M entries, ~1.3 GB output. Run this after copying a fresh releaselist.txt; the script will also auto-rebuild on first use or when the .txt is updated.
